reasoning: The deepseek model conducted a thorough audit of critical files including PKGBUILD, installation scripts, and executable components. All findings were benign standard patterns with no evidence of malicious behavior. The qwen model's skipped status was due to technical failure, not security concerns.
disagreement_analysis: Disagreement stemmed from qwen's audit being aborted due to makepkg download failure, while deepseek successfully completed the audit with safe verdict.
coverage_issues: qwen failed to review any files beyond PKGBUILD due to source download failure
Neither model reviewed .install hooks (none exist in package)
Deepseek prioritized scripts but didn't review all possible network-access files
learnings: Improve makepkg failure resilience - retry downloads or provide better error diagnostics
Prioritize review of files referenced in PKGBUILD functions over development tooling
Enhance file selection heuristics to include package hooks and install scripts first
Add validation for skipped audits to prevent incomplete security judgments
